# Decisions

## Auto-evolve observer uses deterministic ID hashing for idempotent deduplication

[Trigger]: When designing how the observer deduplicates patterns across runs. [Detail]: Observer generates pattern IDs deterministically by hashing the pattern summary + project_id + date. This ensures the same pattern detected in multiple runs produces the same ID, preventing duplicate auto_evolves rows. [Consequence]: Non-deterministic ID generation creates duplicate rows with different IDs but identical content, polluting the UI and breaking deduplication logic.

_Source: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-12-auto-evolve-activation-design.md`_

_Tags: `backend`, `architecture`_

## Observer uses warm-up confidence cap to prevent premature auto-promotion

[Trigger]: When the observer first starts running and begins detecting patterns from accumulated events. [Detail]: Observer enforces a temporary warm-up confidence cap (e.g., 0.80 instead of 0.85) for the first 24 hours to allow patterns to stabilize before auto-promotion. After 24h, the cap is lifted and normal thresholds apply. [Consequence]: Removing the warm-up cap causes low-confidence patterns to auto-promote in the first day, creating brittle rules that are reverted soon after.

_Source: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-12-auto-evolve-activation-design.md`_

_Tags: `backend`, `architecture`_

## Knowledge extraction and scan pipelines simplified without effort parameter

[Trigger]: When tuning knowledge extraction quality or considering configuration surface area for the extraction system. [Detail]: The team removed the { effort: 'max' } parameter from both post-ingest extraction (extract.js line ~406) and cold-start scan (scan.js line ~166). This simplifies the code by eliminating per-pipeline effort tuning and reduces configuration complexity. Both pipelines now call callClaude with only (prompt, model) and rely on default Claude behavior. [Consequence]: If knowledge extraction quality degrades, the only recourse is to upgrade the model (haiku → sonnet → opus) rather than adjust effort. Effort-based tuning cannot be restored without code changes to both callClaude and its call sites.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `config`_

## Knowledge extraction effort parameter removed from Claude invocations

[Trigger]: When modifying knowledge extraction (extract.js) or cold-start scan (scan.js) to tune Claude CLI invocation parameters. [Detail]: Both src/knowledge/extract.js and src/knowledge/scan.js were passing `{ effort: 'max' }` as an option to callClaude(), but the actual Claude CLI spawn args never included an --effort flag. The parameter was accepted by the callClaude wrapper but ignored during invocation. After review, the effort option was removed entirely from both callClaude invocations and the wrapper's conditional arg-building. [Consequence]: Removing unused parameters simplifies the code and avoids confusion about what parameters actually affect the Claude CLI invocation. If effort tuning is needed in the future, it should be added explicitly as both a config parameter and a CLI arg.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `api`_

## Plan generation implementation confirmed 10-step pattern end-to-end across schema, config, helpers, routes, and frontend

[Trigger]: When implementing multi-layer features that span database, configuration, queries, routes, and frontend.
[Detail]: The plan generation feature followed the documented 10-step pattern (schema migration → fresh config helper → pipeline tracking helper → query functions → timeout tuning → unit tests → implementation → route tests → integration tests → frontend → config keys → UI → full test suite). Each step built reliably on prior work with no blocking dependencies, and TDD phases caught the buildPlanPrompt truncation edge case early.
[Consequence]: Complex multi-layer features should follow this systematic pattern to ensure coordinated changes and catch integration issues before production.

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`_

## Plan generation uses spawn() instead of execFileSync to avoid blocking Fastify event loop

[Trigger]: When designing async Claude CLI invocations for long-running processes like plan generation. [Detail]: The plan generation spawns a detached child process via spawn() instead of using execFileSync. This prevents blocking the Fastify event loop during 300-600s Opus processing. The parent route returns 202 Accepted with a task ID immediately, and the frontend polls for completion via GET /api/daily-reviews/:id/plan-status. [Consequence]: Using execFileSync would block all concurrent API requests until plan generation completes, degrading server responsiveness under heavy load.

_Source: `src/routes/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `api`, `performance`_

## Plan generation implementation requires coordinating five independent modules

[Trigger]: When adding a major feature like plan generation that spans database persistence, HTTP routing, async spawning, and UI updates. [Detail]: Plan generation touches schema.js (migrations), src/lib/config.js (fresh config), src/db/pipeline-runs.js (updatePipelineRun helper), src/review/queries.js (savePlan), src/review/plan.js (main logic), src/routes/daily-reviews.js (endpoint), and public/modules/daily-reviews.js (UI). Each layer must be implemented and tested independently, then integrated. [Consequence]: Skipping any layer leaves dead endpoints or orphaned UI; coordinating all five prevents partial implementations and ensures end-to-end verification.

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `frontend`, `architecture`_

## Plan generation spawns detached async process instead of blocking HTTP response

[Trigger]: When designing an async Claude CLI invocation for plan generation from daily review suggestions. [Detail]: The plan generation endpoint returns 202 Accepted immediately with a task ID, then spawns a detached child process with { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' } to invoke the Claude CLI asynchronously. This prevents blocking the Fastify event loop (Claude processing can take 300+ seconds on large contexts). The detached process tracks progress via pipeline_runs table and persists results to daily_reviews via savePlan(). [Consequence]: Clients must poll /api/daily-reviews/{id}/plan-status to retrieve completion status and results instead of waiting for a synchronous response.

_Source: `src/routes/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `api`_

## Plan generation uses stdin piping instead of -p flag for large prompts

[Trigger]: When plan generation invokes claude CLI with large prompts (500+ KB of handoff context). [Detail]: The -p flag passes the prompt as a command-line argument, which fails silently when prompt exceeds ~100 KB due to OS argument size limits (ARG_MAX). The implementation uses execFileSync with {input: promptText} to pipe the prompt via stdin instead. This bypasses OS_ARG_MAX and is compatible with --tools '' flag for single-turn enforcement. [Consequence]: Using -p flag with large prompts causes silent truncation; switching to stdin piping requires removing the -p flag from the args array.

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`_

## callClaude timeout extended to 120s for plan generation with large prompts

[Trigger]: When plan generation invokes callClaude() with large handoff prompts (500+ KB of daily review context). [Detail]: The default callClaude timeout is 60s. Plan generation increases it to 120s via opts.timeout_ms = 120000. Large prompts with accumulated knowledge entries and project configs require extended processing time on Opus. Without the extension, callClaude times out mid-analysis. [Consequence]: Timeout cutoff causes partial or garbled plan output, requiring manual re-generation.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `config`, `performance`_

## Knowledge extraction no longer uses --effort flag

[Trigger]: When tuning knowledge extraction quality or modifying the callClaude wrapper in src/knowledge/extract.js.

[Detail]: The knowledge extraction pipeline (extract.js) and cold-start scan (scan.js) previously passed { effort: 'max' } to callClaude(), which added --effort max to the Claude CLI invocation. This flag was removed entirely from both files on 2026-04-12. The callClaude() function in extract.js still contains conditional code to handle opts.effort if present, but no callers pass it anymore.

[Consequence]: The --effort flag is no longer used in any knowledge pipeline. The conditional logic in callClaude (lines ~205-206) that handles opts.effort is now dead code and can be cleaned up if effort-based tuning is never needed again.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `config`_

## Plan generation uses callClaude helper instead of execFileSync to respect timeout configuration

[Trigger]: When implementing long-running async Claude CLI invocations that might exceed default timeouts. [Detail]: Plan generation calls callClaude() from extract.js instead of execFileSync() directly. The callClaude wrapper respects the config-driven timeout value (default 120s) and returns {output, stderr, duration_ms} for instrumentation. execFileSync was avoided because it would require the caller to handle timeout logic separately. [Consequence]: Using execFileSync directly bypasses config timeout and makes the operation untestable (stderr parsing becomes caller responsibility).

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `config`_
